purpose: Route requests to the narrowest shipped top-level Labs21 template skill while refusing to claim moved or external families that no longer belong to the suite router.
selection_order: Continuity and reflective control beat artifact creation because they decide whether work should continue, compact, or be challenged before more output is produced.
Prompt-artifact work beats skill and project routing when the deliverable itself is the prompt.
Skill-package authoring beats project and delivery routing when the requested artifact is a reusable skill package.
Project founding beats software delivery when the user still needs a staged blueprint rather than execution-stage lifecycle guidance.
Startup pressure testing comes after blueprinting because a teardown is only honest once there is a concrete commercial thesis to challenge.
If the request falls outside the shipped suite boundary, do not force a Labs21 route.

children.name: context-compaction
children.summary: Creates a compact continuation snapshot for long sessions, handoffs, or context-window pressure.
route_when: The user wants a handoff, continuation summary, compacted session state, or context reduction.
The main need is preserving session truth across compaction or session switches rather than doing the underlying task.
avoid_when: The user wants a new plan, implementation change, or domain analysis rather than a continuation artifact.
The request is a confidence check or retrospective without any need to compact context.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this top-level skill before routing when the runtime does not already have the Labs21 continuation helper available.

children.name: self-cognitive
children.summary: Runs confidence checks, retrospectives, and repeatable-workflow extraction when the main need is explicit verification or learning.
route_when: The user wants a confidence check, preflight verification, postmortem, retrospective, or reusable workflow extraction.
The main need is calibrated judgment under risk rather than building the underlying artifact.
avoid_when: The user still needs the core work performed rather than reviewed or extracted into lessons.
The request is really about software-delivery stage selection or prompt design rather than reflective verification.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this top-level skill before routing when the runtime does not already have the Labs21 verification helper available.

children.name: meta-prompting
children.summary: Designs or repairs prompt artifacts such as system prompts, prompt templates, rubrics, and prompt architectures.
route_when: The requested deliverable is the prompt artifact itself.
The user needs prompt architecture, system-prompt structure, rubric design, or prompt evaluation guidance.
avoid_when: The user already has a sparse generation prompt and only needs it enriched, not architected.
The request is about creating a reusable skill package rather than a prompt artifact.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this top-level skill before routing when the runtime does not already have the Labs21 prompt-architecture helper available.

children.name: prompt-augmentation
children.summary: Expands a sparse text, image, or video generation prompt without redesigning the surrounding prompt architecture.
route_when: The user already has the subject and wants richer wording, clearer direction, or a few useful prompt variants.
The request is specifically about augmenting a generation prompt rather than redesigning the whole prompt system.
avoid_when: The user wants a system prompt, prompt template, rubric, or prompt architecture artifact.
The request is about skill packaging, project planning, or software delivery rather than prompt wording.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this top-level skill before routing when the runtime does not already have the Labs21 prompt-enrichment helper available.

children.name: create-skill
children.summary: Creates or upgrades a reusable leaf skill package, including portable guidance and packaging structure.
route_when: The user wants a reusable skill package, skill scaffold, or portable leaf-skill rewrite.
The package's main job is one repeatable workflow rather than family routing.
avoid_when: The package's main job is routing among child skills with explicit metadata.
The request is about using or editing a prompt artifact rather than authoring a reusable skill package.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this top-level skill before routing when the runtime does not already have the Labs21 leaf-skill authoring helper available.

children.name: create-router-skill
children.summary: Creates or upgrades a router package whose main job is selecting among child skills with explicit metadata and honest fallbacks.
route_when: The user wants to create or upgrade a router skill, family entrypoint, or child-inventory package.
The package needs explicit child metadata, selection rules, install hints, or truthful fallback behavior.
avoid_when: The package is a single leaf workflow and does not need family routing.
The request is ordinary prompt design or project planning rather than reusable router-package authoring.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this top-level skill before routing when the runtime does not already have the Labs21 router-authoring helper available.

children.name: project-founding
children.summary: Routes staged product, project, or internal-system blueprinting into the right founding lane when the team still needs a plan for what to build and what to defer.
route_when: The user needs staged blueprinting, covered-now versus later scope, roadmap versions, wedge selection, or founding choices before implementation starts.
The request could plausibly fit more than one founding lane, including general versus AI-native or agentic founding.
avoid_when: The user mainly wants a harsh business viability teardown rather than a staged blueprint.
The request is about implementation-stage software delivery rather than founding what to build.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this top-level family router before routing when the runtime does not already have the Labs21 founding family available.

children.name: software-delivery
children.summary: Routes non-trivial software feature work to the right delivery stage across discovery, delivery-control design, plan review, evaluator selection, and readiness reflection.
route_when: The request is about delivering a non-trivial software feature and the next stage inside that lifecycle is not obvious yet.
The team needs discovery, harness design, plan review, or strict frontend-acceptance routing before implementation proceeds.
avoid_when: The user is still deciding the founding blueprint rather than the delivery stage for an already-chosen product direction.
The request is a simple repo edit or debugging task that now falls outside the shipped Labs21 suite boundary.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this top-level family router before routing when the runtime does not already have the Labs21 delivery family available.

children.name: startup-pressure-test
children.summary: Runs a harsh startup viability teardown using realistic CAC, retention, monetization, burn, and runway pressure.
route_when: The user wants an investor-style critique, launch stress test, or commercial survivability verdict for a startup or business model.
The main need is a cold-eyed economic teardown rather than a staged product blueprint or implementation plan.
avoid_when: The user still needs help defining what the project or product should be before a teardown is honest.
The request is about prompt artifacts, skill packages, or software delivery rather than commercial viability.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this top-level skill before routing when the runtime does not already have the Labs21 startup-teardown helper available.
